mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate on a sustained basis, mechanically supporting present-value valuations of future cash flows and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns while rates remain pinned near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22% as of 2009-02-01 vintage, effectively zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant positive mean versus the full-sample baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while rates remain near zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above an 8% threshold with rapid month-over-month acceleration, labor market deterioration is already priced into equities as forward-looking markets anticipate trough conditions, so subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns turn positive as the labor lag effect resolves.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 8.0% with month-over-month increase exceeding 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.1% vintage rising to 8.3% revised as of 2009-02-01
regime_hint: recession trough with labor market lag
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0% with similar acceleration, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or not significantly different from the unconditional baseline distribution.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in the rate-of-decline of industrial production alongside stabilizing (less negative) payroll losses signals the trough of a production-employment contraction cycle; this joint stabilization pattern, not either series alone, predicts a positive inflection in S&P 500 returns over the following month as recession-bottoming expectations get priced in.
candidate_trigger: 3-month rate of decline in INDPRO and PAYEMS both narrow relative to prior 3-month rate of decline
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised sharply down to 88.57 from 101.337 vintage, reflecting deep contraction as of 2009-01-01
failure_condition: The joint deceleration signal fails if 20-day forward SPX returns following joint stabilization events are not higher, in a statistically significant sense, than returns following continued joint acceleration of decline, across a rolling 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-housing-shock recovery regime, housing starts bottoming near multi-decade lows (below 500K units) signal exhaustion of the residential investment collapse; this trough acts as a leading indicator for broader demand stabilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as construction-sensitive sectors re-rate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 466K vintage / 490K revised as of 2009-01-01, near historic lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following HOUST readings below 500K units are not significantly positive relative to baseline, or HOUST continues declining below prior lows without any subsequent equity rebound within 2 quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.5%, the marginal signaling value of further rate cuts vanishes and short-horizon equity volatility compresses as rate-driven repricing uncertainty is resolved, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.22% as of 2009-02-01, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following FEDFUNDS crossing below 0.50% show mean below zero or no significant difference from unconditional 5-day return distribution (p > 0.05) across multiple episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) directly reflects deteriorating corporate earnings capacity and consumer income, producing a persistent negative drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as long as payroll losses continue without deceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declining from 133768K vintage to 133318K revised as of 2009-02-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns during periods of sustained PAYEMS decline are not significantly negative relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) jointly signal an accelerating consumer demand contraction that outpaces either series individually; this compound weakening predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the very short term as earnings guidance risk intensifies ahead of quarterly reporting.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline exceeds 1% while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised down to 328376M from 346810M vintage, UNRATE rising to 8.3% as of 2009-02-01
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following joint RSAFS decline and UNRATE increase are not significantly negative relative to baseline, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS-only signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep industrial recession regime, a large downward revision to INDPRO relative to its initially reported vintage value indicates markets were previously underestimating the severity of supply-side contraction; once the revised trough is reflected in equity pricing, the information gap closes and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter turn positive as forward guidance resets to a lower, more sustainable base.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised down to 88.57 from vintage 101.337 as of 2009-01-01, indicating severe downward data revision
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following large negative INDPRO revisions (vintage minus revised exceeding 5 index points) are not significantly positive relative to baseline across multiple recession episodes.
